summary.intent: Fix a 404 error that Linux users encountered when selecting an Ollama model in the web UI, caused by hostname divergence between discovery and probe requests, missing fallback to Ollama's native API, and un-humanized error messages.
summary.outcome: All five planned changes were implemented and merged via PR #618: localhost URL alignment, Ollama-native probe fallback, JS error humanization, Rust 404 error type, and full test coverage. All 345 tests pass and the PR was reviewed and committed.

repo: Ollama model discovery uses localhost:11434 (KnownProvider definition in crates/provider-setup/src/lib.rs:910) but the OPENAI_COMPAT_PROVIDERS entry in crates/providers/src/lib.rs:1397 was using 127.0.0.1:11434 — these can resolve differently on Linux with IPv6.
normalize_ollama_api_base_url in crates/providers/src/lib.rs was private (fn) and needed to be made pub(crate) to be reusable from openai.rs.
The project uses axum Router with multiple routes for multi-endpoint mock servers in tests.
CUDA-related build failures appear on macOS when running clippy without excluding the CUDA crate — pre-existing issue unrelated to changes.
Pre-existing rustfmt issues exist in lib.rs and openai.rs in lines far from the changed code — fmt check fails at a repo level but is clean for the specific changes made.
The project uses a 'beads' issue tracker (bd-615 format) separate from GitHub issues.

code.finding: OPENAI_COMPAT_PROVIDERS Ollama entry base URL — changed from http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1 to http://localhost:11434/v1 to match KnownProvider definition.
probe_chat_completions() — after 404 response, checks if provider_name == 'ollama' and falls back to probe_ollama_native() which POSTs to /api/show with the model name.
try_parse_known_error() match block — added 404 arm between 429 and 500 that returns a model_not_found error type with translation keys.

humanizeProbeError() — the initial 404/not-found check was too broad and would have swallowed the actionable 'ollama pull <model>' hint from native fallback errors; fixed after PR review to only match the 404 case specifically.

workflow: Run clippy scoped to only changed crates (cargo clippy -p <crate>) to avoid pre-existing failures in unrelated crates blocking CI assessment.

The Greptile bot caught a subtle JS logic bug (overly broad 'not found' string match swallowing actionable error messages) during PR review — worth checking humanization logic carefully for substring collisions.
biome check --write handles JS linting/formatting for the web assets.
just format-check / just lint / just test are the standard quality gates in this repo.

friction: Pre-existing rustfmt violations in lib.rs and openai.rs cause 'just format-check' to fail at the repo level even though the new code is correctly formatted — required manual verification that changed lines were not in the diff.
CUDA crate build failure on macOS made blanket clippy runs noisy; had to scope clippy to specific crates.
No Dolt remote configured on the worktree, so 'pull beads updates from main' silently failed — needed to ignore and proceed.

Required multiple passes of reading openai.rs (at least 8 separate reads across different line ranges) to understand the full structure of probe methods and test patterns before being able to write the fallback code.
open_items: Pre-existing rustfmt violations across lib.rs and openai.rs should be cleaned up in a separate formatting PR.
Pre-existing clippy errors in moltis-httpd crate are unresolved.

Manual QA verification (Ollama running with a real model, and with a non-existent model) was listed in the plan but not confirmed completed in the transcript — relies on reviewer or CI environment with Ollama.

The 'not found' substring in humanizeProbeError was fixed to avoid swallowing the ollama pull hint, but the exact final condition should be verified to ensure it only matches the OpenAI-compat 404 path and not other error messages containing 'not found'.
